posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Moicano vs. Duncan
Renato Moicano, TKO, R3
Chris Duncan has exceeded expectations thus far in his UFC career but if we're being honest a lot of the guys he's beaten have proven to be trash. Moicano atleast has some decennt ranked names on his resume and has been the main event a couple of times. The former training partners thing always makes for a weird fight but I think Duncan's style is one that is never going to make it 5 rounds. I see him succumbing to the damage by round 3 and getting finished here.

Jandiroba vs. Ricci
Virna Jandiroba, DEC
Piss break fight.

Shahbazyan vs. Park
Jun Yong Park, DEC
Love this fight. First round KO merchant vs durable guy who gets better the longer the fight goes on. Im picking Iron Turtle 1. Because he's awesome and 2. I dont see Edmen finishing him in one round. I'll take Turtle by split decision.

Bekoev vs. Gore
Azamat Bekoev, TKO, R1
Get Gore off the damn roster what are we doing?I

McMillen vs. Zecchini
Tommy McMillen, DEC
Italian prospect = easy win. I think Mcmillan is kind of **** too but obviously UFC wants to build him up before feeding him to the wolves. I think the idea is to give him an opponent he can finish but I doubt he can do it.

Estevam vs. Ewing
Rafael Estevam, DEC
People picking Ewing are still living in the moment of his debut. Estevam may be boring but he's a 14-0 fighter who's 3-0 in UFC despite solid competition. I'm taking the more proven and battle tested guy here.

posts.author: okazy
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Moicano vs. Duncan
Renato Moicano, SUB, R4
Underdog pick here and I'm going with Renato Moicano. Renato Moicano has beat Drew Dober with ground work and heavy control, he finished Jalin Turner with punches from mount, and dominated Benoit Saint Denis in the first round enough for them to stop the fight. Moicano also submitted Brad Riddell in the first round. Chris Duncan has already shown us he can win by submission, wrestle and push a pace by winning strike heavy fights in a 15 minute fight. His wins over Rebecki and Ashmouz clearly show that. I'm picking Moicano because Duncan is vulnerable in grappling exchanges and he gets dragged into messy fights due to him being chaotic and dangerous which caused him to get subbed by torres. Moicano is way more controlled than him and will find the right moment to get the back and get a submission. Round 1 is always the feeling out process and this is a five round fight which is why I'm not picking Moicano Submission by Round 1,2, or 3 but instead by Round 4.

Jandiroba vs. Ricci
Virna Jandiroba, DEC
Picking her to win because she has a stronger winning style in this matchup against Ricci. Virna's goal is to get takedowns and get very long control time. In the Marina Rodriguez fight she had 12:02 of control time and in the Loopy Godinez fight she had 6:35 of control time and 3 submission attempts. Virna beat Yan Xiaonan by getting 3 takedowns and 9:59 of control time. The red flags in Ricci's game was which she faced Loopy Godinez where she went 0 of 6 on takedowns while getting outlanded and against Yan Xiaonan she was outstruck pretty badly and couldn't get that much control time.

Yakhyaev vs. Ribeiro
Abdul Rakhman Yakhyaev, TKO, R1:
I'm picking Rakhman Yakhyaev by KO/TKO in the first round because Brendson Riberiro hasn't shown tough durability in his fights. In all of Brendson's losses he has lost 5 times by KO/TKO in the first round which is why I made this pick.

Estevam vs. Ewing
Rafael Estevam, DEC
I'm picking Rafael Estevam By Decision. His whole wrestling and control game is basically all he has and he is consistent with how he wants the fight to go. His pattern and path to winning rounds is more repeatable.

McMillen vs. Zecchini
Tommy McMillen, SUB, R2
I'm picking Tommy because he has shown he can win multiple ways which are by submission and a 3 round decision win over David Mgoyan. Tommy landed 113 of 218 significant strikes compared to Mgoyan's 85 of 190 and Tommy did a very good job and did a lot of work in the third round. Manolo has danger due to his flying knee KO against Abou Toukara, but losing to Morgan Charriere is a warning sign even though it isn't a bad loss. Manolo landed 9 of 41 significant strikes and Charriere landed 26 of 49 and Manolo got stopped because of kicks to the body at a distance. Tommy has the style to pressure and use his grappling threat to control this fight. I expect Manolo to have his best moments in the first round and then Tommy takes over in the 2nd round and finds the submission.

Ruchała vs. Delano
Jose Delano, DEC
I'm going with Jose Delano here because out of Delano and Ruchala, Delano has the sharpest stirking of the two. Delano's sharp striking showed against Manuel Exposito where he landed 146 of 222 significant strikes (65%) in a 15 minute fight and it shows he literally showed dominance in the striking. In that fight it shows very huge efficiency and huge volume. When he fought against Abu Muslim Alikhanov he also showed his ability to defend takedowns and was able to get the finish. Ruchala is definitely more battle tested of the two but he is very easy to hit cleanly. In the Gomis fight the weakness that was shown was that he can lose rounds to a striker that is very clean outside and that also includes even when he gets some control time. This will be very important because Delano looks like a very accurate striker who can punish that weakness. I'm not picking a finish to happen because Ruchala is very durable which showed in the Eskiev fight that he can survive dangerous situations and get momentum and take over the fight as it goes on. I'm trusting in his durability that he is tough and has excellent cardio to make it over 3 rounds.

Petersen vs. Pat
Pat Guilherme, DEC
I'm going with Pat Guilherme by Decision because he is very consistent in his fights when it comes to his plan to win which is controlling the range, being the more clean striker, and having excellent accuracy. He has beaten people that were very pressure and grappling heavy and was still able to win minutes and show he has the ability to still win despite his opponent having control time. Thomas Petersen's path to win is to pressure Pat so he can wrestle and get takedowns to get control time and the fights he has had against Mohammed Usman and Don'Tale Mayes show that. Petersen has the better resume obviously, but when he is able to get the wrestling and control time going he looked pretty good. If he doesn't and his takedown gameplan fails he is very hittable in the striking in the losses against both Petrino and Gaziev. So basically I trust Pat to keep the fight standing which is where he has the advantage in and win in a 3 round fight.

Costa vs. Nicoll
Alessandro Costa, KO/TKO, R2
I'm going with Alessandro Costa by KO/TKO in Round 2 because he has a better resume than Stewart Nicoll. Costa has faced people like Amir Albazi who has a win over Kai Kara-France and Steve Erceg who has had a five round title fight against Alexandre Pantoja and a five round fight with Brandon Moreno. Despite that Costa is inconsistent he is still able to show how dangerous his finishing ability is by stopping Jimmy Flick with 65 of 91 in significant strikes (71%) and also by stopping Kevin Borjas. In Nicoll fights he has shown a pattern where he puts on the pressure and tries to get takedowns and top control so he can get his ground and pound finishing going. There is a clear red flag in Nicoll which is him getting submitted by Jesus Aguilar in the first round and also getting outlanded badly by Lucas Rocha and also doing terrible with his takedowns going 2 of 20 which is exactly why I'm going Costa KO/TKO. I choosing Round 2 instead of Round 1 simply because Nicoll will start fast in Round 1 and try to get his pressure and takedowns going. After Round 1 the fight will become much better for Costa to get a KO/TKO as things will not go well for Nicoll in the later rounds (we saw this with Lucas Rocha vs Stewart Nicoll).

Vannata vs. Flowers
Lando Vannata, DEC
I'm picking Lando Vannata By Decision because he has already shown he can win fights with high output when it comes to striking, also has way cleaner distance striking, good leg and body work, and has very good takedown defense to survive heavy pressure when it comes to wrestling. The win he had over Yancy Medeiros shows that since he was very strong when it came to his accuracy and volume in the striking and in the fight against Mike Grundy he has already shown he can be able to handle a guy that is very takedown heavy and still be able to win the fight due to being active. Lando's resume is pretty good as his wins over Yancy and Grundy are good and his losses to Green, Zellhuber, and Charles Jourdain aged very well as losses to not be ashamed of as they were legit opponents. If Darrius Flowers does win it will be by Submission in Round 1. Darrius will show early danger in this fight like he has done in his other fights, but when the fight gets longer he usually gets outworked, gets controlled, or even gets finished. I trust Lando way more in a 15 minute fight than I do Flowers.

Cowan vs. Pereira
Alice Pereira, DEC
I'm picking Alice Pereira by Decision even though Hailey Cowan clearly has the stronger resume and her losses aged well against people like Nora Cornolle and Jamey-Lyn Horth. Alice does have a way better cleaner style for this matchup specifically simply because she showed striking in range, very good accuracy in a 15 minute fight, and good head work. Hailey has shown repeatedly that her vulnerability is when a fight is very messy or when her opponent is just more cleaner and efficient when it comes to striking. She was submitted by both Kelly Clayton and Nora Cornolle and was also outstruck in a 15 minute fight against Jamey-Lyn Horth. I think Alice will be way more likely to be able to keep this fight on the feet where it favors her and win more minutes in each round.

Bekoev vs. Gore
Azamat Bekoev, TKO, R2:
I'm picking Azamat Bekoev by KO/TKO in Round 2 because he has way better win quality compared to Tresean Gore. Azamat Bekoev wins over Zach Reese and Ryan Loder have aged well when compared to Tresean Gore's best win which is Josh Fremd. Trocoli is another win Tresean Gore had but it doesn't meet Bekoev's win quality. Azamat Bekoev based on his fights has proved more that he is an person that finishes fights early. Bekoev knocked out Zach Reese and Ryan Loder in Round 1. The only way I can see Tresean Gore winning this fight is by catching a guillotine on Bekoev's takedown attempt. Bekoev has a respectable lose against Yousri Belgaroui who just showed Mansur Abdul Malik levels and KO/TKO him in Round 3 so it is nothing to be ashamed of. I'm picking KO/TKO in Round 2 instead of Round 1 because in the Marco Tulio fight he wasn't overwhelmed until Round 2 started where Tulio took over and got the KO/TKO over him and Tulio is a legit opponent.

Barbosa vs. Gatto
Melissa Gatto, DEC
Melissa Gatto vs Dione Barbosa is a very close fight. I'm picking Gatto because she looks like the more complete fighter over 3 rounds. She has good striking to be better than Dione Barbosa and enough grappling to survive Dione Barbosa's grappling. Melissa Gatto isn't only a striker she can wrestle people aswell. Resume wise Melissa Gatto losing to Tracy Cortez isn't a bad look because Tracy Cortez has some wins and losses that looked pretty good on her ufc record and those are when she had fights with people like Jasmine Jasudavicius (beat her clearly), Rose Namajunas (went five rounds with her), and Viviane Araujo (who she dominated in that fight). Barbosa's path to win this fight is her submission threat and the danger that will come in the early grappling moments. If she gets her grappling going early and gets a takedown and top control she will win rounds pretty fast and can also find a submission. The weakness she will have in this fight will be her striking cause if her grappling gets taken away from Melissa Gatto her offense is very limited. Some examples are when Dione Barbosa faced Ernesta Kareckaite where she won that fight but went 0 of 6 on takedowns in round 3 which isn't a good look in the later rounds (2/11 on takedowns that whole fight). So I expect Gatto to survive the grappling Barbosa will give her in the 1st round and then take over in the 2nd and 3rd round and win a 29-28 decision against Dione Barbosa.

Yakhyaev is a beast and Ribeiro isn't very good. We've seen Ribeiro get outstruck multiple times, and also outwrestled to a ground and pound so Yakhyaev is just better everywhere. Likely KO since Ribeiro's striking defense is so bad, but wouldn't be surprised if he gets a club and sub like last fight. He jumped on the back quickkkkk

Bekoev vs. Gore
Azamat Bekoev, TKO, R2
I'm not really sure what to think about Gore's chin. Brundage basically put him out with one punch but it took Tulio like 100 shots to finish him, and Tulio is definitely a better striker than Bekoev. I feel there's a good chance this fight goes to a decision, considering the fact that Bekoev likes to mix in the wrestling and it's hard to finish guys with ground and pound. If Bekoev just keeps the fight standing for 3 rounds though, I think he can finish Gore in 2

posts.content:
[Predictions]

Petersen vs Pat
Petersen DEC

These are some of the most baffling odds of the week to me. I understand Pat looks legit on paper but he is a textbook fraud. Sure, he’s got the frame, athleticism, and youth but the skills are not there. Pat’s record is insanely padded and he never has clean performances; he’s almost always dropping a round or struggling before finding the win. His debut was fairly dominant but not impressive at the same time; all he did was spam the jab like usual and his horrendously outmatched opponent had no answer for it. Pat’s fights outside the ufc suck as well, legit all he does is spar with his opponents and spam the jab. He never deviates from it on the feet and everything is set up with the jab. To his credit, it is effective given his reach, but it also becomes insanely predictable and it’s also the only thing he throws. Pat has no killer instinct at all and will drag a dead corpse to the finish line; he did this in his debut and was very close to taking that can in LFA the distance. Pat is hittable af, has bad cardio, and is a negative grappler; his grappling is not ufc level and bordering on fraudulent. There’s this one fight of Pat’s against a 0-0 guy that I recommend everyone watches. Pat got dropped by this guy, taken down at will, death gassed after 8 minutes, and came very close to losing against a 0-0 guy who hasn’t fought since. I’m ****ting all over Pat and it’s not like Petersen doesn’t have flaws of his own, but there is a big gap between the two imo. Petersen is a “bad” fighter but for a HW he is very serviceable; it aint pretty but I’d say he’s one of the better unranked HW. Petersen is a capable striker with good power, and he’s a very solid wrestler. Any good athlete with either good tdd or better striking will cook Petersen, but I think he’s a great gatekeeper for this division. His striking is clunky and rudimentary but he hits hard and will pressure. I see the jab of Pat causing a lot of issues for Petersen here; since he doesn’t move his head and reacts badly to being hit. We saw in his debut that Petersen had no answers for the jab and honestly got pieced up. That being said, there is a difference between Pogues and Pat; Pogues may be a nothing burger but he has good boxing. Pat will land at will but the problem is that he never does anything other than jab. Pat also has bad tall man’s defense, so he could easily get dropped himself. I do favor Pat on the feet but imo he’s really bad at separating and doesn’t have a deep skillset on the feet, so I wouldn’t be shocked if Petersen can even things up. The reason I like Petersen a lot here is because of the wrestling. Petersen’s wrestling is nothing special but for a HW its very solid. What I like about Petersen is that he knows exactly what he’s good at and won’t deviate. He is going to shoot from bell to bell and when he gets tds with little resistance, he can chain wrestle for 15 minutes no problem. Pat cannot wrestle at all and is a white belt on the mat; he has no resistance to the td and is a turtle once put down. Petersen is without a doubt the best wrestler he’s ever fought, and I don’t think Pat is ready for the takedowns. He has looked atrocious any time he’s been put on his back, and Petersen is a big step up in this regard. I can never pick Petersen by sub after that atrocious showing against Mayes, but it wouldn’t shock me if he finds one here. I expect Petersen to have a lot of control time here, and I think he makes this one look very easy. Maybe even gets a finish.

Costa vs Stewart
Costa KO RD 2

Another loser goes home fight here, flyweight is always fun though. Both of these guys have grappling backgrounds, although Costa likes to strike. Costa’s bjj is very good imo but he has poor wrestling and doesn’t shoot that much as it is, instead preferring to brawl. Nicoll is a solid grappler himself but the problem is that’s all he has. I liked Nicoll on regionals because he would show a lot of crafty things, but sadly he’s not ufc level. His game is too limited and for as solid of a grappler as he is, even it isn’t that good. He was winning against Aguilar in his debut but struggled to hold him down and then got subbed; I know that’s his signature move but for a supposed high level black belt you really should not be getting caught in that. The Rocha fight showed me Nicoll doesn’t belong here. I think Rocha is decent but he has a clear hole in his tdd yet Nicoll had no success getting him down and didn’t really have an answer once Rocha defended the initial tds. Nicoll’s striking is not ufc level and when he can’t score tds he doesn’t offer anything. He showed crazy toughness against Rocha but Nicoll also got his ass beat and was reacting very poorly to everything that landed. I think Costa is pretty bad himself, as well as possessing a ton of habits that won’t get him far. His last fight in particular was an awful look; I think Coria is a great prospect but Costa came in with an awful gameplan and once he did finally grapple, he broke his toe and promptly quit afterwards. I’ve broken a toe before and I know how much it hurts, but it was crazy how the second he broke it, Costa made zero attempts to fight through it. He let Coria reverse him and just stalled on his back until he quit in r3. Watching the tape back on Costa, he’s really quite bad. He has great hardware and is explosive, which is how he’s won most of his fights but at the ufc level he’s got a really bad process and doesn’t really wrestle that much either. When he isn’t using his grappling he’s got power, but he’s also low volume, very hittable, and super clunky; he’s also got a really bad chin and has been hurt in almost all of his ufc fights. Luckily for him, Nicoll has no striking threat at all, nor does he have the footwork to stop Costa’s rudimentary, plodding style. Costa has powerful leg kicks which we saw Rocha have some success with last fight, and I think he’s the better boxer as well. Nicoll doesn’t have the power to get his respect, so I think Costa will be able to walk him down. Both are good grapplers but I don’t think we see a ton of it here. Costa has shown some awful looks when put flat on his back, but to his credit both times (Coria, Albazi) came from him getting dropped, so he was hurt. Costa is a black belt himself and has a pretty good scrambling game, good tdd, and is hard to hold down. He may have broken his toe quickly, but he hit a pretty insane reversal to back take on Coria. Erceg also mounted him at one point but Costa fought through every position without panic. Nicoll could cause some issues if he can flatten Costa out, but I think getting him there will prove difficult. He doesn’t have the wrestling to get Costa down (especially due to his stocky frame) and even if he does, I think Costa will be able to scramble and keep Nicoll off of him; we’ve actually seen Nicoll is a step behind in most scrambling exchanhges at the ufc. He couldn’t hold Aguilar or Rocha down for very long at all, and almost immediately they resisted his grappling. I really don’t like a lot about Costa’s game, but I think this is a great matchup for him. Nicoll is one dimensional and Costa has the grappling/tdd to neutralize it. Nicoll showed good heart and toughness last fight, but I think Costa can get him out of there. Rocha could’ve finished that fight a few times but didn’t fully commit. I think Costa has better killer instinct and hits harder, so he could punish Nicoll. Either way I think Costa wins this one fairly easily.

Vannata vs Flowers
Vannata DEC

I unironically love this fight. Loser goes home bout between two of the most random and irrelevant guys in the division; icl I thought both had been cut at this point, and both haven’t fought in forever. Lando is one of the strangest fighters of all time, he’s honestly got a deep bag of skills and a very flashy style yet he hasn’t done jack **** since the Makdessi KO. His career has been marred with ****** performances where he completely gassed out or didn’t show up. Lando has also been MIA for close to three years and bouncing around weight classes, so who the hell knows what he’s going to show up like. I legitimately thought he’d retired, and while he’s been in the lab I’m not expecting much from him here. He’s 34, a tweener, and hella inactive which is all a recipe for disaster. Flowers on the other hand just sucks though, he is a regional fighter in the ufc who has more than overstayed his welcome. Flowers is only here because of that injury KO on dwcs, he has no skills other than being explosive while early. His three ufc fights have been embarrassing showings, and 155 is a terrible move for him; Flowers can barely make weight and when he shows up, his explosivity is completely gone nor can he fight the same way since he’s so scared of gassing. Lando is a smaller 155er but this is definitely his best weight class. He’s still very flawed but he doesn’t gas as bad and I think he just performs better at 155 overall. The striking is weird here but I favor Lando. He’s got a very unique striking style that not many guys can replicate; it’s like a weird, quasi-karate style where he keeps his hands low and bounces in/out of range. His movements are extremely slick and he’s got some very cool spin attacks, but there’s no substance behind any of it. He looks flashy to start but against better guys they quickly figure him out and expose Lando for his bad cardio and bad defense; he gets hit soooo much after r1. It’s partially a cardio thing but also mental too, he seems to freeze up and fall apart. That being said, I think he’s the better striker here. Flowers legit has nothing to offer besides early explosions. He may blitz Lando early but outside of that I don’t think he has anything to offer, Flowers has no striking process and is just a low level guy in general. He was also 1-10 (7 ko’s) as a pro boxer and as I mentioned, can’t even fight the same at 155 because he’s killing himself to make weight. Just because it’s Lando, he could get dropped and ko’ed randomly. Outside of r1 though, I expect him to style on Flowers. I know that’s risky to say considering it’s Lando, but he has historically owned this level of competition badly; I’d even say Flowers is on the same level as say Marcos Mariano. Outside of an early bonk, I expect Lando’s weird movement and kicking to throw Flowers off. Flowers is going to sit back and swing haymakers with no setup while Lando picks him apart at range. I wish Lando would grapple more as it’s an easy path to victory here. Flowers is not a ufc level grappler and has nothing to offer once you take him down. Lando doesn’t wrestle but if he ever feels like it, all he has to do is shoot once and Flowers will fall apart. This line seems pretty wide considering how inconsistent and weird of a fighter Lando is, but at the same time there is a pretty stark difference in skills. Lando is a “bad” ufc fighter but there’s a difference between being a mediocre guy who stays around for a decade, versus getting signed off of circumstance. Flowers is a regional guy who got lucky to get in, where as bad as Lando is, is at least a serviceable ufc fighter and a long time vet. I expect Lando to handle business here, Flowers is a jobber.

Cowan vs Pereira
Cowan DEC

Is this the most apex fight ever? Loser goes home, unranked W135 screams Apex Classic. This fight is gonna be awful but from a breakdown perspective I do find it kind of interesting. To me, Cowan has a lot of the same flaws/skills as Alice, as well as their entire gimmicks being the same (heavily reliant on their size/athleticism to win fights). I am not a fan of either of these girls; I never liked Cowan prior/after DWCS, and Alice was one of the most baffling signings of recent years. UFC was weirdly trying to push Alice as the next big star, for reasons completely unknown. Both girls have looked very bad in the ufc but imo there is a clear difference between the two. Cowan is a “bad” fighter by all intents and purposes, but she’s also won a round in both of her UFC fights; both girls are also fringe top 15 so while not the greatest fights, these aren’y exactly layup fights either. Alice, on the other hand, does not belong here. She had 0 legitimate opponents prior to the ufc and looked godawful against a nothing burger in Rendon. The whole gimmick around Alice is that she’s supposed to be some super athlete, but she doesn’t even leverage that well; with Cowan, she at least somewhat knows how to wrestle and uses her athleticism to get to the punch quicker. With Alice, she doesn’t even have a process; she moves around and throws out random feints to stay busy, but theres nothing behind her shots and she doesn’t really understand the nuances of striking. Against Rendon, she spent the entire fight circling the cage and doing herky jerky movements while getting pieced up. Cowan is a bad striker herself but at least she’s serviceable, Alice legit has no setups or process on the feet; she just flails her arms around and hopes something will land. I guess she has some okay pop in her hands but nothing special. Cowan is a very bad striker herself but it’s moreso having to do with her defense. Her offense is fine, and her striking technique is definitely better than Alice’s. Cowan’s issue is that her head never moves off the centerline and she doesn’t know how to adjust; she’s extremely hittable as a result. Luckily for her, this may not matter because again, Alice legitimately possesses zero striking technique. This is not going to be an all striking affair though, Cowan will mix in grappling. Her actual bjj is horrendously bad, but she is an okay wrestler and at least aggressive enough to force something. She has scored tds in all of her ufc/dwcs fights and even took a round off of Cornolle with some effortless control in her last fight. When Cowan gets reversed or is forced to be the nail, she completely falls apart. She has no idea what to do when you get on top/on the back and panics hard. However, I’m not sure this is going to be much of an issue as Alice has no grappling whatsoever. She doesn’t wrestle in any of her fights and when Rendon put her on the mat she was lost; even nearly getting subbed. Alice is big and athletic so I am worried she could reverse position on the mat, but even then I'm not sure she possesses enough bjj skills to even threaten anything on bottom. Cowan is not a good wrestler/grappler by any means, but she is at least confident enough to mix it in every fight; I feel quite confident that Alice has nothing to offer on her back, and I also feel pretty confident that she will not be able to stop the 2nd/3rd/etc takedowns when they come. I want to cut her some slack since she’s only 20 and had a debut on a big card, but that was not a performance that told me Alice is going to last long/have any potential in the ufc. I think this matchup is honestly harder than her debut as well, as the two positives Alice has going for her (size/athleticism) won’t even be beneficial here as Cowan possesses those same intangibles. Cowan at least has some skills while Alice entirely uses her athleticism as a crutch. There are a lot of red flags surrounding Cowan like her health issues, weight issues, injuries, and her pedo husband (look it up), but at the end of the day she is the more experienced girl, can match the athleticism/size of Alice, and fight a smarter game. I don’t think Cowan is good at all but I’m not sure Alice has the striking or grappling to one up her. Alice doesn’t have any crafty things up her sleeve either, as she’s an extremely green prospect who really shouldn’t even be here. There is a difference between coming up short against actual good fighters (Cowan) and getting outclassed because you don’t belong in the ufc (Alice). She didn’t show me a single positive thing in her debut that makes me want to back her in this spot.

Bekoev vs Gore
Bekoev KO RD 1

This is a layup to get Bekoev back in the win column. He looked pretty awful against Yousri but as we’ve seen, Yousri may just be a complete problem after all. I’m still a little unsure what to think of Bekoev as his pre ufc tape was not the greatest, and he showed a lot of his old self against Yousri. This is a complete push over though, I don’t even know how Gore is getting another fight. Gore honestly has some good hardware and athleticism, but he has no idea how to use any of it. He also just never should’ve been here, making it to the finals of the lowest level TUF season ever and somehow staying around. Bekoev is going to blitz him from the jump like he’s been doing, and I don’t think Gore can handle that. Bekoev’s striking is not pretty and the Yousri fight showed that he’s very plodding, but I’m not entirely sure that will be an issue here. Gore is very hittable and won’t be able to stop the pressure I think. Bekoev should be able to land on him at will. Gore could land back since he does have some power and Bekoev is very hittable/plodding, but I don’t think he will be able to handle the volume coming back his way. Bekoev is going to spam tds as well but honestly im not sure how successful he will be. Bekoev has a very explosive blast double but the problem is that’s kind of all he has. His chain wrestling is not the best imo and his takedowns are kind of reliant on his explosivity. For all his flaws, Gore actually has pretty good tdd, strong balance, and good hips, so it’s not that easy to get him down. I do expect Bekoev to score takedowns here but I wouldn’t be shocked if Gore defends most. Even if he does, Bekoev just needs to close the distance so he can start whaling on Gore. Even if Bekoev is struggling to get a td early or is getting hit, Gore has really bad cardio so he just needs to wear on him. Like all his prior ufc wins, Bekoev also has a gigantic edge in experience, which I think will go a long way here. Even if he’s somehow struggling he’s seen significantly more things than Gore and can use that to his advantage. I expect this to be a snuff film, Gore does not belong here.

Barbosa vs Gatto
Gatto DEC

This is honestly a pretty solid wmma fight. Gatto is making her bi-yealy appearance after going ghost and Barbosa is coming off of a competitive fight with Karine. Barbosa is a bit of a weird fighter; she’s a lot better than her record shows but at the same time has a lot of flaws that have led to that 8-4 record. I will say, she has taken a lot of really hard fights on short notice, which has led to that shaky record. This is a really weird one to call, this feels a lot like Barbosa’s last fight; Gatto isn’t nearly as physical as Karine but she has very similar habits of high level bjj but awful fight iq and a tendency to pull guard. Barbosa’s last fight was insanely close, as she would get outgrappled and then reverse position, but Karine did just enough from bottom to get the nod. Gatto is a very high level bjj player but god damn she is the biggest bjj nerd of all time. She is way too comfortable off of her back and while she has a crazy active guard, you cannot win from your back in 2026 unless you find a submission. That being said, Gatto is going to keep Barbosa on her toes at all times with her bjj attacks. She does have a bad habit of playing guard but I also think her control is a lot better than Karine’s; Gatto has a nasty body triangle. She will give up positions to go for subs which I really don’t like, but I do think she can outgrappler Barbosa. One thing about Barbosa’s last fight was that she had more control but wasn’t doing enough with it; she would sit in guard while Karine spammed subs from her back. This was not the best look to me because Gatto will do the exact same thing. She will flop to her back but once she does so, her guard is insanely active. I really hate how she will relegate herself to being on her back (it’s how she lost the very competitive fight with Cortez), but I can see her doing enough to force Barbosa to play defense. This probably comes down to who gets top control first, I think Barbosa is a much better wrestler but I think she will have a difficult time stopping the bjj attack here; I think this is going to be very similar to her last fight. If it stays standing, I like Gatto here. Barbosa is a serviceable striker with some decent power (despite 0 ko’s) but she is low volume and hittable. Gatto is rangy and uses that well. Weirdly enough every UFC win she has is by KO, despite not being a power puncher at all. She has a doctor’s stoppage and two body punch ko’s. I also thought she got robbed against Lipski, winning the first 10 minutes pretty clear imo. This proves that she can hang with better strikers on the feet. I think she can land more and has the durability edge on the feet. Her fight iq really bothers me but with Barbosa’s style I think there will be openings for Gatto to win minutes. I don’t like this layoff for Gatto either but tbf, she’s had multiple throughout her career and is still young so it’s not the end of the world. Her skillset is honestly pretty underrated, I feel she has Barbosa covered in most areas; just comes down to her fight iq, she cannot play guard off of her back all fight.

posts.content:
[Predictions]

Moicano vs Duncan
Moicano SUB RD 2

Good fight but really questionable main event choice; I guess they really had no other options. I’ve always been a Moicano guy but the gimmick has run its course and he’s been looking quite washed as of late. Part of the reason I’ve enjoyed backing Moicano so much is because he’s always been underrated. He still is in this spot, but as he gets older a lot of the flaws that made him underrated in the first place, are starting to rear their ugly head again. I personally don’t hold the Benny fight against him as I think any version of Benny is a bad matchup for Moicano, but he looked awful there. He gassed hard and got completely shown levels in every aspect of MMA. He had some awesome momentum building and even though the Islam fight isn’t much of a loss given the circumstances, I’m really worried that Moicano will never gain that back. That being said, I think this is a good fight for him to win. Duncan should obviously be the favorite but I’m not sold on him being a ranked guy. I have to give credit where it’s due, he clearly is a workhorse and went from a bum to legitimately very good. He’s clearly on the ATT supplement plan as well, as he never gets tired anymore and is shredded to the bone. That being said, I still don’t like some aspects of his game. I can appreciate that he’s a just bleed fighter, and honestly that strategy could be very successful here, but at the same time Duncan still has a ton of the same flaws he came to the ufc with. For example, he still gets way too ****y on offense, and gets rocked/hit all the time. His tdd is still pretty questionable as well imo. He’s clearly not the glass cannon we thought he was from DWCS, but just because he hasn’t been dropped in a while doesn’t mean that this same flaw still exists. Now, I don’t really expect Moicano to do all that much on the feet, but he has always had very underrated Muay Thai so you never know. I hate how hittable Duncan is but I think his striking style is a nightmare for Moicano. Moicano is very chinny and does not like being pressured at all. He can land on Duncan but unless he drops him with the jab, Moicano is going to have a hard time getting respect. Duncan may not be a clean striker but he can easily ruin Moicano’s day by just walking forward and throwing. If Duncan wins I am expecting him to KO Moicano, but funny enough Duncan is not the crazy KO threat eveyrone thinks he is. He absolutely hits hard but he has 0 ko’s in the ufc and only 2 knockdowns. His last fight was close to a KO tbf but point still stands; even if he hits hard he is not the one punch ko guy Campbell made him seem like. Duncan is putting in work at ATT and I know he will be prepared for the grappling, but I still really doubt his level there personally. Duncan’s tdd is really bad (50%) and half the time he doesn’t even bother to defend. He loves to pull that damn guillotine and has no problem putting himself into bad spots. I really doubt he catches Moicano in that and I am genuinely worried for Duncan on the mat. I know Moicano is washed but hes still a very high level grappler. Beneil made him look bad but we have to remember Beneil is a credentialed grappler and has only ever been outwrestled like twice. Every other fight prior to his L streak, Moicano was completely dominant once on top. He may be washed but personally I don’t expect this to change. Duncan has improved but nothing he’s done has shown me that he can defend the takedown and escape the back take. Duncan’s get ups are good but he also hasn’t fought any grapplers like this before. I’ve said it every fight since it happened but seeing Oki ragdoll him before the finish was a really bad look to me. Duncan’s only ufc loss is a back take to rnc as well, which is the Moicano signature. To be fair, Duncan did nearly KO Torres with the first shot he threw, as well as getting dropped before getting subbed, but I didn’t like how he got manhandled on the mat. It is worth noting that Torres is a different monster physically, but he literally ragdolled Duncan with little effort and subbed him quick. I can’t fault Duncan for fighting whoever the UFC offers, but his wins don’t really inspire confidence either imo; everyone save for his last two have losing UFC records and are cut/on their ways out, and the McKinney/Rebecki fights were not flawless showings either. He went life and death with Rebecki, and had a case to lose imo. He did beat McKinney but not after going through a few minutes of hell himself. These are obviously very different styles than Moicano, but it is worth noting how big a step up this is, even with Moicano being washed. Duncan is in his prime but he’s also turning 33 next month, so it’s not like Moicano is being fed to some young, super prospect. If this goes past 10 minutes I expect Duncan to break Moicano late. However, I think his willingness to take risks will get him in trouble here. While fresh, Moicano is a top level grappler and has a nasty back take. He may be on a downturn but he has tons of experience and has fought significantly better/more dangerous guys than this. He may sadly be shot, but I think he can keep the gate this one last time.

Jandiroba vs Ricci
Ricci DEC

I just may be the biggest Ricci hater in the world, but I weirdly like this matchup for her quite a bit. Virna is a very tricky girl to beat but I also think she’s a little overrated because of the run she had. No doubt she’s a phenomenal grappler - maybe even the best pure grappler in the division, but her title run consisted of all strikers and no iq Loopy who decided it was a good idea to wrestle Virna the entire fight. Virna has some elite top control and submission game but she’s also fought a ton of girls who don’t have any tdd and willingly grapple with her. Ricci may be willing to grapple with her too, but she easily has the skillset to win this fight imo. Firstly, Ricci is very hard to take down and has a huge strength/athleticism edge; for all I dislike about her, she is a very good athlete and extremely strong. She uses it as a crutch a little, but at the same time she’s also become a top 10 girl despite a mediocre skillset because she’s so strong/athletic. Being old isn’t the be all end all in WMMA, but Virna turns 38 next month and is already pretty slow/clunky to begin with; I’m worried we may see some physical decline out of her. She’s already at a disadvantage because of this, but Ricci is also extremely hard to take down and has a great track record against grapplers. To be fair, she’s fought nobody on the level of Virna, but she did face a similar style in Robertson and beat her soundly. Of course, very different styles despite the identical flaws/gameplan but the fact Ricci chose not to grapple and knew to strike tells me she will be prepared for this one as well. Even when she has been taken down, she’s never been held down longer than 40 seconds; so she is good at getting to her feet. Ricci is a black belt herself so I don’t think she will be worried about the craftiness of Virna. I also really liked to see the evolution in her game last fight; Ricci still dropped r1 pretty clear imo, but she was stopping the tds of Ribas and doing a great job of punishing her in the clinch, which led to the finish. This will be huge here imo, because I expect Virna to fail on a lot of tds (she’s not a great wrestler to begin with), and if Ricci is punishing her for this, she will quickly run out of options. I don’t like Ricci’s striking but she has the clear edge here, Virna is not a ufc level striker and looks so lost when forced to strike for long periods of time. Ricci is only 1.5 years removed from the embarrassing Yan fight, but that was too much too soon. I really don’t like Ricci but I am weirdly very confident she gets this one done. I think she has the perfect skillset to beat Virna, and she has a lot of Virna’s weak points covered.

Yakhyaev vs Ribierio
Yakhyaev KO RD 1

Another layup for Yakhyaev. Ribierio is a good test for prospects because he’s super dangerous but also very easy to finish. If you are too ****y or not careful, he can bonk/sub you out of nowhere. I think Yakhyaev is a top tier prospect for this weight class; he’s shown that he’s got very powerful striking but can also wrestle very well. He did have some bad looks early in his career but like I said, he’s improving at a very fast rate. When he debuted he was primarily a wrestler but since training in Germany his striking has hit a new level; he is dropping everyone he fights, usually inside of a minute. Yakhyaev has fought a lot of cans recently but that win he had way back against Kovalev has aged very well. It was not the prettiest fight but Yakhyaev went 25 minutes and outwrestled a guy who was on the Russian national judo team, so that's cool. Yakhyaev should win this however he wants, I assume he will probably grapple due to the size and danger threat of Ribierio, but he could easily KO him too. Ribierio has a glass jaw and has been ko’ed by some incredibly weak shots on multiple occasions. Yakhyaev has serious power so all he needs is one shot to end it. Yakhyaev did show some bad looks on bottom in his debut, but I think he should be okay here. All he has to do is avoid the meme kimura sweep here and he’s good. Ribeiro cannot wrestle at all and is a fish on his back. The only thing I’m split on is what method Yakhyaev finishes this in, but I expect another quick and dominant finish out of him. I know he’s fighting cans but I personally think Yakhyaev will be a top contender in a few years. He’s got a deep bag of tricks and is insanely young.

Estevam vs Ewing
Ewing KO RD 3

Best fight on the card by a mile. Ewing is coming off of one of the best upsets/performances of 2025 and Estevam is being forced up to 135 after 3 failed attempts to make 125. This is a pretty binary fight on paper, Estevam wins if he gets takedowns and Ewing wins if he keeps it standing. I think the odds should be closely lined since Estevam is the best grappler Ewing has ever fought, but after looking at Ewing’s career in depth I think this is a very good matchup for him. His tdd is not perfect, and I’ve seen Ewing taken down on multiple occasions. However, he’s very hard to hold down, has very good bjj (he was a grappler as an amateur), and has shown great urgency to get up. Estevam is a very legit black belt and he can really overwhelm you on the mat. I do expect him to get takedowns here but I’m not so sure his style will translate well at 135. Estevam was a huge weight bully at 125 and used that to his advantage since he is a grappler. At 135 against bigger guys, I don’t think he will be as efficient since guys are about the same size if not bigger. Even besides this, Estevam’s wrestling wasn’t as dominant as I remembered. He scores a lot of takedowns but the control time on said takedowns is actually not super impressive. I remember being very impressed when he ragdolled InnerG in his debut, but many likely forget that InnerG in 2022/3 had serious issues with anyone who wrestled him; he only recently fixed this issue. His other two performances did not impress me that much, both were stylistic layups and he just kind of cruised. He couldn’t hold Aguilar down for very long on any one attempt, and death gassed hard. He actually bothered to make weight for this fight too, so the fact he gassed even with a “good” weight cut tells me that his cardio is simply bad, I don’t think an extra 10 pounds will change much. Estevam doesn’t have the cardio to support his style either, he goes ape **** insane with td after td, and once on the mat he stays very active. He was fine against Bunes but that’s because Bunes is a guard player with no tdd, Aguilar and Johnson made him work and Estevam just slowly started to burn himself out. This is another reason why I think the move to 135 is bad, if he was exhausting himself working for takedowns, he’s gonna have to work even harder against bigger guys. Ewing can be taken down but one thing I saw on tape is that he is a very good scrambler and immediately looks to get to his feet the second he gets taken down. The Brand fight was a bad look in this context, but it’s also important to remember Brand is a pure Muay Thai fighter, so Ewing likely was not expecting wrestling. In all his other regional fights, Ewing’s tdd has looked on point and he’s even dominated a few guys on the mat himself. Ewing has great balance and really strong hips which help him a lot on the mat. As long as he avoids the takedown, I think Ewing is going to smoke Estevam; the striking is a complete mismatch. Estevam’s technique is borderline okay, but he is awful in extended combinations. He has terrible body language and shells up when tired. Ewing is a super high level striker with insane power and pace. He immediately puts you on the back foot in every fight and is super dynamic. Despite being the true BW, Ewing has a significant speed advantage here, as well as being a fantastic athlete. I think this will hurt Estevam a lot because Ewing has faster hand speed than anyone he’s fought before, as well as having tons of power. One thing about Ewing’s striking that I think is key here, is that Ewing immediately pressures in every fight. All he does is constant forward pressure, and I think this could be very beneficial in taking away the td threat. Estevam is still going to shoot, but if you get him going backward, the td becomes much more clear to see/stop. He also lacks the striking to deal with pressure, so Ewing could really cook him. Besides his normally very high pace, Ewing will work the body heavily; this is key for a gasser in Estevam. Estevam came very close to getting finished by both Aguilar and InnerG, but they were either too gassed or too scared of the td to capitalize. The difference here is Ewing will throw tons of combos if Estevam is hurt, and he has amazing killer instinct. Stylistically Estevam has a POV, and I do think Ewing’s tdd could be better, but I think he has the skillset to get it done here. Besides the move to 135 lowering his skill, it’s also worth noting that Estevam has lost a round in every single UFC fight. He is undefeated but he’s also extremely beatable. I picked Ewing to beat Wellmaker (sorry, I had to flex that) and I personally think he is a top level BW prospect. Estevam is good but I think he will hit a wall here.

McMillen vs Zecchini
McMillen SUB RD 1

Predatory matchmaking right here. I will say, McMillen is very raw so he should be fighting cans, but this is a whole other level of can crushing. I thought Manolo was retired, dude got signed as cannon fodder nearly 4 years ago, got owned badly, and is only now coming back. I knew he was bad when I taped his debut but he was even worse than I had remembered; I don’t even know what he’s supposed to be good at. He’s a striker but his entire game is just brawling and windmilling his hands around. He’s not a very good striker nor is there much depth to his game either; it’s all boxing. He’s very hittable as well and doesn’t protect his chin. His grappling is among the worst I’ve ever seen, he has clearly never wrestled a day in his life. He has bad tdd and an even worse game off of his back. It seems like he’s been in the lab training since this layoff, so I’m not sure what that’s all about. This is a layup for McMillen to get a highlight reel finish, the only issue I have is by what method; I think KO and SUB are both live, and I would be shocked if this left the first round as well. I do like what I’ve seen and that Mgoyan win is going to age very well, but McMillen still has a long way to go. He’s clearly very talented but I honestly struggle to find his ceiling. He has some great qualities like his massive frame, really good cardio (or at least the will to push through being tired), and very high level wrestling/scrambling. Mgoyan is a very high level wrestler and Tommy was able to negate the majority of it with his scrambles and his favorite position, the front headlock. Tommy’s striking is very bad imo but he at least makes it work with his zombie pace; I have major concerns against real strikers but it was very impressive seeing him walk Mgoyan down and break him with his pace. Tommy was exhausted too but you could see he wanted it more; that’s a skill you can’t teach, and is another reason I think Tommy has some upside. His striking technique is awful but I’m not sure that matters here since Manolo sucks too. He can crack so I wouldn’t be surprised if Manolo does some crazy **** and drops him, but I would be incredibly shocked if Tommy got slept; he showed great recovery against Mgoyan so even with bad defense, I think he’s got a great chin. Tommy surprisingly doesn’t offensively wrestle that much, so I'm not sure if I should pick SUB or not. If he shoots a td this fight is over, Tommy will easily show levels on the mat; whatever reservations I have, it’s clear as day he can wrestle/scramble his ass off. I think what probably happens here is Tommy comes out guns blazing (no pun intended), hurts Manolo bad on the feet, and he will panic shoot and give the neck up; 90% of Tommy’s finishes on regionals came from panic shots where he held front headlock and used that to get a guillotine. Either way, Manolo is a tomato can and I expect Tommy to handle business. Maybe there’s some oh **** moments with his risky style, but he’s clearly the better fighter here.

Ruchala vs Delano
Delano DEC

Pretty brutal second fight for Ruchala. Personally I don’t think super highly of him but they really aren’t doing him many favors with this one; Delano is one of the best, and easily most UFC ready guy from the last season of DWCS. I’m honestly a little underwhelmed with Ruchala to be honest. He fought good comp in KSW and has plenty of champ experience but his style is just meh to me. He’s a decent striker but very passive and low volume; his game is extremely kick heavy and that’s almost entirely what he throws. He has some okay boxing but it’s all singular shots at a time. I really don’t like Ruchala’s boxing defense either, he’s pretty stiff and has been cracked numerous times; including the fight right before UFC. Ruchala is a serviceable striker but against legit comp I’m not really sure what to think; he doesn’t hit that hard, is very passive, very low volume, hittable, and content to be on the backfoot. I can’t take much from the Gomis fight as that was a super difficult debut and very tricky style, but he looked mediocre there. He did rock Gomis with a head kick in r3 but other than that he mostly stayed at range looking very confused. Again, this is par for the course against Gomis, but seeing Ruchala’s other fights I don’t think he’s that special. He especially doesn’t possess the striking skillset to give Delano problems (imo). To beat Delano you either need to aggressively spam tds for 15 minutes or get in his face and force a war out of him. The former is quite difficult to do as Delano has insane tdd and great get ups, but the latter did happen against Santos. Santos is a monster but he completely buzzsawed his way through Delano; he made it so Delano couldn’t get out of first gear and combined pressure/takedowns to slowly break him. This is a good loss but it was a very onesided bout; kind of makes me think Delano is not the best nail. That being said, I don’t think this will be relevant here, as Ruchala doesn’t bring that kind of pressure, nor does he have the boxing/wrestling to make Delano uncomfortable. Delano is a very high level MT striker and is a sniper. He doesn’t have a ton of KO power but he’s very accurate and throws with bad intentions. He has a very deep bag of tools on the feet and fights at a high pace while mixing in strikes to all parts of the body. I honestly could see a KO here, I know he somehow decisioned Expositio but I don’t think Ruchala is super durable; his reactions when getting hit clean are not the best. I think he will really struggle with the output as well, I’ve never seen Ruchala fight at a high pace and as mentioned, he is a pretty passive counter striker. Ruchala could maybe make it interesting if he gets tds, I was actually pretty impressed with his grappling in the Gomis fight; I know he has a bad bottom game but he’s pretty difficult to take down. Ruchala won a round with control and put Gomis in some very bad spots. However, I think Delano has significantly better stand ups and he’s super hard to control, so I don’t think Ruchala will be super effective. I’m not a fan of the price tag here, although I do think it’s accurate. It’s just a little off to me because while underwhelming, I don’t think Ruchala is a complete tomato can like the odds are implying. This is also not the easiest debut ever, although again, I expect Delano to win comfortably. I would not go parlaying him or get fancy with props (he burned me so bad taking expositio to DEC lol), but I expect a clean win and performance from Delano. KO is live but I have buyers remorse from last fight.

posts.author: s00perchungus
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Duncan has a very good bad habit of finding these Hail Mary moments in his fights that just suddenly turn the tide in his favor. For example, his fight with Terrance McKinney was going how those usually go, with Duncan wobbling right up until he managed to place an elbow right on the chin of McKinney and come back. Then with Duncan vs. Oki AND Duncan vs. Vucenic, Duncan was losing on the feet and getting rocked until both Oki and Vucenic decided to go for a takedown, which failed and ended with EACH barely trying to escape a really weird armpit guillotine. I'm also going to bring up his contender series fights because he was getting pieced up in both and was constantly sprawling to survive as he does in classic fashion until he gets his Hail Mary shot his second time around that wins him the fight. However, once faced with another contender like Manuel Torres, Duncan was out quickly because Torres was bringing the heat and kept the gas going while Duncan was hurt and put him out with a choke of his own before Duncan could get going. Now I know Moicano and Torres are two different beasts, yet out of all of the outcomes in this fight, I'm most confident in Moicano just beating the absolute bricks off of Duncan just like he did with Jalin Turner. I think Moicano is going to play a bit of a counter game at first, as Duncan loves to be first when standing, yet he leaves himself open to be countered too much, which flips Duncan into takedown mode, which is his go-to once he knows he's losing on the feet, as seen in his past fights. Yet Moicano will stuff any attempt OR reverse it into a takedown of his own volition, which Moicano can then go for a club 'n' sub or GNP.

posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Cowan vs. Pereira
Hailey Cowan, DEC
Smart by the UFC booking Pereira on this card that nobody is going to watch. She's either going to win a boring low level fight and remain hyped up for whatever reason or she loses and can simply fade into obscurity. I took Rendon to beat her last time and I think Im taking Cowan here. Pereira is just too green and way too low volume on the feet. If she takes a sparring match approach against Cowan its hard for me to imagine her winning on the scorecards. Cowan is a red flag herself considering all of the pulled out fights due to injury, but like genuinely this is a step down in competition.

Barbosa vs. Gatto
Melissa Gatto, DEC
I think both women have had success against opponenets who were good matchups for them and the women they've lost to are all ranked so its hard to judge where their skill level is at. Its a classic striker vs grappler match but I think I like Gatto here just cause she's proven to have good cardio in these fights where Barbosa kind of struggles unless she can lay and pray minutes off the clock in a fight that goes long.

Vannata vs. Flowers
Lando Vannata, SUB, R2
This is such a trash can fight holy moly. Like how do you even make a pick on who to win? Both guys are must fades in like every single matchup. I guess Im taking Lando? He's got more UFC experience and he fights at a better gym with better training partners. Flowers gets subbed in pretty much all of his losses so I predict an early sub here round 1 or 2.

Hope vs. Kamaka III
Kai Kamaka III, DEC
Kamaka was a flop his first run in the UFC but I actually kind of like him to win here. Idk anything about Hope but I can see that this debut is on short notice and he's never had two camps and two weight cuts this close together before. That + the debut jitters is usually a bad recipe for a young fighter going in there with an experienced guy. I like the upset on this first fight of the night.

posts.author: Fast_Tulip
posts.content:

Costa vs. Nicoll
Alessandro Costa, TKO, R2
Stewart is a takedown or bust kinda guy. His striking is very rudimentary and I doubt he will get a UFC win unless he faces a can or someone that continuously accepts bottom position. He's the type of grappler where if they can't get the fight to the mat, they are almost guaranteed to lose. His last fight he wasn't really throwing any strikes at all, just shooting from way out which is way to easy to sprawl against. I would be extremely surprised if Costa lost this, and would be somewhat surprised if he didn't at least get a finish. Duncan’s pace is crazy (almost 12 strikes/min), but he’s not that accurate and leaves openings. Moicano is the opposite — much cleaner, more efficient, and way more dangerous on the ground.

If it stays on the feet, Duncan’s volume could steal rounds or even create a finish.
Leaning Moicano here, probably sub or control-heavy decision — experience + grappling edge feels like the difference.

posts.author: JeanSilvaEnjoyer
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Moicano vs. Duncan
Chris Duncan, TKO, R2
WE believe in Chris Duncan. WE don't care that he almost got finished about five times by Terrance McKinney, then got a jump up to a ranked LW in his very next fight. WE don't care that he looked like a corpse after his fight with Rebecki. WE don't care that he got finished by Manuel Torres and Slava Claus Borschchev. (Actually, that last one is really bothering me, now that I think about it). The fact is, Chris Duncan never quits on himself. People talk about how he doesn't do any one thing super impressively, and that's true. The striking? Decent. The wrestling? Decent. Nothing astounding. No one excellent skill like Moicano has with BJJ. But what Duncan does have is heart, and he's got more of it than almost anyone I've ever seen in this sport. This guy out McKinney'd Terrance McKinney. And that fight with Rebecki? Yes, it was close, but only one of those two guys was face down on the canvas, coughing blood, and about to lose consciousness when the final bell rang. The other one had his hands in the air, and he was Chris Duncan. Of course, Moicano is not going to make this a sloppy brawl like Rebecki did. He's going to go heavy on takedowns from the moment the bell rings. And I do worry that Duncan will go for that guillotine he likes. Admittedly, it's a very good guillotine, but it's not going to catch Moicano. They train together, though, and Duncan has said he's never submitted Moicano, so I think (or hope) he knows that he's not going to Gilly Goose Gang his way to a victory here. I think Duncan is a smart guy. He loves to play dumb and go for a brawl, but fundamentally, I think he's a good game-planner, and he knows what he's in for here. He's on the mat with good people like Grant Dawson for this fight. Moicano, meanwhile, might be planning on a "people must have forgot" type of performance with this fight. I mean, Moicano did almost murder BSD a couple of fights ago. It was perhaps a career-best performance. Plus, Moicano hasn't been on his podcast in months, and he's kind of an arrogant guy, so I could easily see him looking to prove everyone that he's still got what it takes to be a name in the division. I'm not super confident in this pick at all, but I've got to go Duncan. I think he stuffs most of the takedowns or at the very least stays safe enough to not get submitted. Once it's on the feet, Moicano might be the more technical and experienced striker, but he's not going to land with as much thud as Duncan. The crucial thing here is that Moicano is almost 37 years old, and he gassed in one round in that fight with Dariush. Yes, that was a completely different matchup, and Dariush is uber-talented on the mat, but Moicano looked like he didn't even want to be there. He knocked Dariush down in the first round, Dariush's chin is absolutely fried, and Moicano still couldn't find a way to win in the next two rounds. Even though it looks like Moicano is preparing much more for this fight than he did in the previous one, Father Time is not on his side. Duncan by TKO in R2 or R3.

Jandiroba vs. Ricci
Tabatha Ricci, DEC
This card just might be the worst ever for me in terms of picks. I hate this pick. I HATE this pick, but I just got a feeling, man. Jandiroba is maybe the best in the division in terms of pure mat skills, but her striking is clunky and slow, and Ricci is not a good matchup for her stylistically. Ricci might be the most mid fighter ever, but one area where she excels is not getting controlled on the mat. Not even Gillian Robertson could get more than a minute of control time on Ricci. And while Ricci fancies herself as more of a striker than a wrestler these days, I like the fact that she has a good defensive base for this fight. Jandiroba's only path to victory in my eyes is control time and submission attempts, and if she can't get those, then she's kind of screwed. Ricci's striking isn't pretty, but I give her the edge there both in terms of form and thud in comparison to Jandiroba. Finally, there's the if-you-know-you-know factor here. Unless Virna completely shuts out Ricci and makes her look like a fool, there's a very good chance that Ricci gets another robbery decision on the scorecards.

McMillen vs. Zecchini
Tommy McMillen, SUB, R2
You know the UFC are going to do everything possible to protect a hyped prospect from the O'Malley camp. Hence, we have McMalley vs Zucchini.

Ruchała vs. Delano
José Delano, DEC
Ruchala looked decent against William Gomis. They're really doing him dirty despite him being a good fighter by pitting him against absolute dogs. I didn't like the way that Ruchala let Gomis dictate the pace of the fight, particularly because Gomis himself is not a pace-pusher, and it says good things that Delano's only recent loss is against Gabriel Santos, who's showed a lot of promise in the UFC. This could be a lot closer than people are imagining, but Delano's the obvious pick here.

Yakhyaev vs. Ribeiro
Abdul Rakhman Yakhyaev, TKO, R1
Obvious choice

Cowan vs. Pereira
Hailey Cowan, DEC
This fight kills me. Alice Pereira looked god awful in her debut against Montse Rendon. I can't even describe it to you. She was running away the entire fight, just sliding around the Octagon and getting chased down by a lady who may have been billed as almost identical in height and reach, but looked a lot shorter and smaller in the cage. Rendon just got cut, too, and yet when she finally caught up to Pereira, she landed the most goofy-ahh combination you can imagine, nearly KO'd Pereira, and then nearly subbed her at the end of the second. It was pitiful. When Pereira actually got the chance to swang and bang, she just started spazzing and throwing caveman-style overhands, hoping something would land. The form was terrible. Now, she's very green, so maybe she's made huge strides since her last fight, especially since it was a loss on a UFC debut. Plus, Cowan herself is nothing to write home about, and even if Pereira decides to caveman it up, one of those caveman overhands might land on Cowan's chin. However, I would feel like an idiot for picking Pereira after that performance. I just can't do it.

Costa vs. Nicoll
Alessandro Costa, TKO, R2
I'm not high on either of these guys. Both looked pretty pitiful in their previous fights. Nicoll was throwing with some nasty intent on Rocha, but he was coming nowhere close to the target, and even though Costa fumbled badly against Coria, he had a competitive fight with Albazi (before getting finished), and he KO'd Kevin Borjas, which is a decent win for lower-level flyweights. I mean, we just saw Borjas almost slime out Imanol Rodriguez, who's everyone's favorite prospect these days. So, uh, yeah, Costa, I guess.

Barbosa vs. Gatto
Dione Barbosa, DEC
I'm going back and forth on this one. Gatto stays way more active in the striking, and I don't like how Barbosa is content to take someone down and then do a whole lot of nothing on top. She searches for submissions, but she's not going to get one against Gatto in all likelihood, and she did absolutely no ground and pound of any consequence against Karine Silva. In fact, Karine Silva won the fight largely because she was able to get some nasty-looking elbows in on Barbosa from bottom, and Barbosa just sort of sat there and ate them. Gatto stays very active on bottom, so I could see this same dynamic posing a problem again for Barbosa once again. However, Gatto's coming off of a big layoff, and I favor a close fight with Karine Silva along with a decision over Kareckaite better than what I see in Gatto's record.

Bekoev vs. Gore
Azamat Bekoev, TKO, R1
Execution. They're clearly giving Bekoev this fight to save him as a prospect after he was brutally slimed by Belgaroui. Gore, a striker, just got outstruck by Robert Nickal victim Rodolfo Vieira.

Petersen vs. Pat
Guilherme Pat, DEC
Before I explain why I am picking Pat, let me spend a moment trashing him. Guilherme Pat looked terrible in his debut. Yes, he has nice footwork, and yes, he throws with some heat, and yes, he's 6'5", and yes, he defended all the takedown attempts (if you can call them that), but despite the fact that he was piecing up Frye for the entire fight, Pat kept allowing Frye to delicately, politely, gingerly deposit him against the cage in the most slow-motion clinch entries I've ever seen in my life. He showed absolutely no urgency whatsoever to get out of the clinch, to get away from the cage, or to finish the fight. It was a pitiful performance even though it was a victory. However, I am not inclined to ever pick Thomas Petersen to win a UFC fight. He beat Mayes and Usman, perhaps the two lowest-level guys in the division (are they even in the division anymore?). And, yes, admittedly, it's very possible that Pat is not even above Mayes or Usman. It's impossible to know at this point because he's so inexperienced. But Petersen's gas tank is ass unless he's laying on top of you for the entire fight. Pat's gas tank isn't much better, but he's got a huge striking advantage, and I don't think he's just going to get ragdolled in there. Could be wrong, though. As for KO or DEC? It's either Petersen DEC or Pat KO/DEC. I favor DEC because I saw Pat refuse to look for a finish last time around. He seems content to win rounds on the scorecards and then chill against the cage for the rest of the fight. That's a dangerous gambit against a guy like Petersen, but I guess we'll see if he's been working on his counterwrestling.

Vannata vs. Flowers
Lando Vannata, DEC
Dear God. What an abominable fight to pick. I like Vannata, but coming off of a three-year layoff? Darrius Flowers at least has a lot of power early in a fight, and he could easily KO Vannata in the 1st, but if it goes longer than that, I don't favor his chances, so I guess I'll go Vannata. At least he has wins in the UFC back in the Stone Age for us uncs to remember (WHO does that to John Makdessi?)

Hope vs. Kamaka III
Dakota Hope, DEC
What a fight. I have done zero tape study on these two, so I'm at a loss. Kai is the favorite and has 25 pro fights compared to 12, so he has a lot more experience, but they seem to be fighting at roughly the same level of competition, I guess. I've heard talk that Dakota's striking defense is ass, so there's also that to consider, but I'm not loving the idea of picking a guy who's being pulled short-notice into the UFC for a second stint after just losing a splitty to a 2014-era Conor McGregor victim. This seems more like the UFC trying to set up a new prospect rather than reintroduce a crafty vet. The UFC doesn't always get what it wants, but it's a factor to consider.

posts.author: Littledohyo
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Moicano vs. Duncan
Chris Duncan, SUB, R2
I'm willing to be educated on this quite a bit, but I see that they are from the same gym and red flags start going up. Then I remember that Moicano SOLD for that Makachev emergency contract. MONEY MOICANO as a legendary fighter is DEAD. He got what he wanted. He is now firmly a company man, and as much as I like him, he will now DO AS HE'S TOLD YA. Analysis for this fight is not important. Moicano still has the popped collar bone and will until after he retires, by his own word, so he can't be taken seriously as a legitimate fighter any longer. Prove me wrong on this fight big boy.

Jandiroba vs. Ricci
Tabatha Ricci, DEC
UFC wants Ricci to win. So, win condition for Virna is only submission. So, I am picking Ricci because it will probably be a decision. Rooting for Virna though, just like in her last fight.

Ruchała vs. Delano
José Delano, DEC
Watched a few of these guys' most recent fights and had to change my pick to Jose, despite my desire to be le epic fringe picker. I do think the odds are off, especially since Ruchala can absolutely improve and may well HAVE improved after his last fight, which really wasn't as bad of a look as the odds make it seem. It wasn't amazing or anything but he knows well enough what he's doing. UFC throws so many clueless guys into fights against DWCS dudes that this seems kind of interesting for a change. Ruchala could easily get styled on for three rounds, though. I normally fault people for not getting finishes on DWCS because that's literally the time it matters most, but then I remembered the guy Jose was against and that he just wouldn't go away so I can't really hold it against him.

Costa vs. Nicoll
Alessandro Costa, DEC
I didn't look into this at all. I saw guillotine by manlet as a loss in Nicoll's history and I didn't look back. Aint got that dawg innem.

posts.author: ChrisSweeney
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Moicano vs. Duncan
Chris Duncan, TKO, R2
I think this is gonna come down to Duncan’s grit. He’s not gonna respect Moicano’s striking. He kind of respects Moicano’s grappling because they're training partners and he said he could never sub Moicano. And this is a huge fight for both of them. I doubt Duncan goes for a stupid sub attempt that he knows would get him in trouble and keep it on the feet for the most part. And out-brawl a weaker 37 year old Moicano. Duncan does real damage Moicano doesn’t. The BSD performance will never happen again. It was perfect and still he didn’t put him out and BSD fought a decent 2nd round. Give me Duncan to land something heavy and finish it with G&P. R.I.P Money Moicano.

Jandiroba vs. Ricci
Tabatha Ricci, DEC
I hate this pick. I think Jandiroba’s better, bigger, and has the masculine advantage. Even though Jandiroba was 1 round off being champ, styles make fights. Jandiroba can’t strike and Ricci is hard to control. Jandiroba would have to win by control time/sub attempts. And all Ricci has to do is not get controlled, which is her specialty, and force Jandiroba to strike, which is her weakness. And Ricci usually has the sway of the Judges. 29-28.

Yakhyaev vs. Ribeiro
Abdul Rakhman Yakhyaev, TKO, R1
Yakhyaev is one of the best prospects in the UFC. He will get a highlight finish inside 2 minutes. Spinning wheel kick?, Flying knee?, 1-2 That puts him down?, G&P?, Sub?, Who knows.

Estevam vs. Ewing
Rafael Estevam, DEC
Ethan Ewing fans are some of the most ridiculous people I know. It was clear that outside of Wellmakers right hand he had nothing else in his toolbag. After it landed, Ewing ate it. It was wraps. Welcome to a real UFC fighter. Every fight Estevam is the worse striker, it's nothing new. I’ve seen Ewing taken down before and Estevam is good and persistent at it and a serious threat on the ground. If InnerG can get controlled and taken down by Estevam, why should a newcomer who beat a one trick pony striker? I think Ewing’s ok on the ground, smart, has good bodywork, good combos. Is the better striker obviously but lacks in the TDD against dudes not on Estevam’s level. The move up to 135 should help Estevam's cardio as well.

Ruchała vs. Delano
José Delano, DEC
My 2nd favorite prospect off DWCS. Jose is a world-beater, He has a Great stiff jab, Head movement, Footwork, Power, Cardio, Timing, Placement, Bodywork, Good defense, Good ground game, Great TDD, Good get ups. Like Lerryan he was Great in LFA, Really showed out on the Contender Series. Ruchala was cool on KSW but he’s kinda low volume and gets hit. I rate Gomis so the loss wasn’t that bad. Ruchala is a decent fighter honestly they aren’t helping him with the matchups but Jose is here to change the division. KO is also very live. Ruchala will be open to get countered, Delano is a sniper.

Petersen vs. Pat
Guilherme Pat, TKO, R3
6 '5, Athletic, Good striking, Good power, Good TDD in his debut atleast. If Petersen holds him against the cage I know Pat showed no urgency to get off last fight but he beat Frye up even in the clinch. If Petersen can’t damage him I just see him getting pieced, trying to smudge, and getting pieced until Pat KO’s him late or they both gas and Pat wins by just being able to throw strikes. Petersen beat Mayes and Usman the two lowest level guys in the division who showed close to no threat. And Petersen has a trash gas tank and isn’t dangerous himself. At least Pat can strike and stop a TD.
