Gore is a headcase and usually shows up to cash the check. This will be his last however when Bekoev puts him out with the quickness in the 1st. Fighter Removed: Tresean Gore

Hailey Cowan vs. Alice Pereira
Hailey Cowan via Split Decision
I literally can’t wipe the memory of Alice’s last “fight” out of my memory and she’s got fraud wins which doesn’t help matters. Cowan is dreadful and frankly I hate we’re even having to talk about this fight. So we won’t much further, Cowan takes a greasy Split and yea…next fight.

Lando Vannata vs. Darrius Flowers
Lando Vannata via Decision
I honestly had no idea Lando was still signed until this fight was announced. Last time I checked he was a roofing contractor or something like that and boom, he’s got one more in him. I’ve always liked him even if he’s not very good per se, he brings a unique style and flair. Flowers, you can catch the shuttle to the airport with Tresean. Lando takes the decision and *ahem* Fighter Removed: Darrius Flowers

Alessandro Costa vs. Stewart Nicoll
Alessandro Costa via 2nd Round TKO
Costa is getting a very favorable matchup coming off a knockout so he should give Stewie the business here. Unless they give Nicoll a softball at some point, he might not be long for the UFC. Costa puts him down with a flurry of punches in the 2nd.

Thomas Petersen vs. Guiherme Pat
Guiherme Pat via Decision
This card is cursed with decisions, I can feel it in my loins. Thomas the Tank Engine is starting to leak oil and Pat is at least a functional Heavyweight; albeit he’s not snatching gold anytime soon. This fight going the distance will drag the vibes down but thems the breaks sometimes. Pat takes the…you guessed it…the decision victory.

Robert Ruchala vs. Jose Delano
Jose Delano via 3rd Round TKO
Somebody’s gotta break the decision vibes, right? Ruchala is going to be largely cannon fodder in the UFC and Delano should have the advantage in the striking as the fight wears on. He’ll hurt Ruchala late and get the 3rd round stoppage.

Tommy McMillen vs. Manolo Zecchini
Tommy McMillen via 1st Round Submission
Another guy I had no clue was on the roster until this was announced and T-Mac sends him packing in the 1st with a sub. Fighter Removed: Manolo Zecchini

Rafael Estevam vs. Ethyn Ewing
Ethyn Ewing via Decision
I’m so glad I can fade this unprofessional jabroni. I don’t care if he wins his fights, your job is to make weight and he’s a habitual weight misser. My one concern is he’s a weight bully and can use it against Ewing here but **** him, he’s a cheater and he’s not getting rewarded for it. Ewing takes the decision and Estevam should be punished by getting lectured by Jon Anik about professionalism and making weight. Make him feel like **** Jon.

Abdul Rakhman Yakhyaev vs. Brendson Ribeiro
Abdul Rakhman Yakhyaev via 1st Round KO
This might need a “Viewer discretion is advised” warning before they start this because Yakhyaev is going to do disgusting things to Ribeiro. Not only does Brendson fold with ease but Abdul is legit and ferocious. He just mauls you and puts you in The Turkish Device and you just want to go home. Don’t worry Brendson, it’ll all be over before you know it because Yakhyaev destroys your existence within a few minutes.

Virna Jandiroda vs. Tabatha Ricci
Tabatha Ricci via Split Decision
I didn’t like Virna’s comments this week about essentially being boring and that being ok as a fighter. She was eluding to Charles and his way in winning the BMF Belt and frankly, nobody wants to hear that. Tabby’s cool and she’s getting better but I don’t put a ton into that Ribas fight because Amanda’s pretty much done as a fighter now. Virna might be losing a step at this stage and if Tabby pushes the pace, I’ll take her to edge Virna on the scorecards.

Renato Moicano vs. Chris Duncan
Chris Duncan via 3rd Round TKO
I think we all slept on Chris Duncan and he’s making us pay for that. Actually, he’s about to make Money Moicano pay for that and probably get a bonus for this too. Moicano was a good company man stepping in when needed but frankly his time in the sun is up and Duncan will outclass him until he finishes him in the 3rd.
